mission.title: Install and Configure ExternalDNS
mission.description: ExternalDNS is an operator that dynamically manages DNS records for Kubernetes resources, allowing services and ingresses to be discoverable via public DNS servers. It is particularly useful for cloud-native applications that require automatic DNS record updates based on Kubernetes resource changes. Key trade-offs include the need for proper DNS provider configuration and potential delays in DNS propagation.

steps.title: Add the ExternalDNS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ExternalDNS Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add external-dns https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

helm repo update

```

> This repository contains the ExternalDNS Helm chart.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install ExternalDNS with Helm
steps.description:
This step installs ExternalDNS in the specified namespace with the necessary configurations.

```bash

helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.21.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

```

> Replace 'aws' with your DNS provider as needed.
commands: helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.21.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource to demonstrate how ExternalDNS manages DNS records.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.kubernetes.io/v1alpha1
kind: DNSRecord
metadata:
  name: example-dns
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  domain: example.com
  endpoints:
    - name: example-service
      service:
        name: example-service
        port: 80
EOF

```

> Replace 'example.com' and 'example-service' with your actual domain and service.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.kubernetes.io/v1alpha1
kind: DNSRecord
metadata:
  name: example-dns
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  domain: example.com
  endpoints:
    - name: example-service
      service:
        name: example-service
        port: 80
EOF

steps.title: Verify ExternalDNS is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the ExternalDNS deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

```

> You should see the ExternalDNS pod in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ExternalDNS
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ExternalDNS installation and may result in data loss of DNS records managed by ExternalDNS.

```bash

helm uninstall external-dns --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete crd dnsrecords.externaldns.kubernetes.io

```

> This will remove the ExternalDNS deployment.
> This will remove the Custom Resource Definition.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ExternalDNS
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up the current configuration, upgrading the Helm chart, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kube-system > external-dns-backup.yaml

helm upgrade external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.21.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

```

> Backup current ExternalDNS configuration.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the ExternalDNS pod is running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.21.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to update DNS records'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the DNS provider credentials are incorrect or missing.

**Fix:**
Verify the credentials and permissions for the DNS provider configured in ExternalDNS.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No endpoints found for service'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified service does not exist or is not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the service name and ensure it is of type LoadBalancer or has the correct annotations.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'DNS record already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there is a conflict with existing DNS records.

**Fix:**
Manually check the DNS provider for existing records and resolve conflicts.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to misconfiguration in the ExternalDNS deployment.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system' for detailed error messages.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Azure DNS and improved AWS Route 53 integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No special migration steps required for upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the ExternalDNS pod in the Running state with the command 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
